its stream with peace-persuading sound , That steals beneath the fading foliage pale , Or , at the foot of frowning crags upreared , Complains like one forsaken and unheard

sublime Above the clouds of this tempestuous clime
of thousands echoing to the skies

thy wild banks and still retreats

prevent That mars the silent current of content
The crosses and the cares that sometime fret Life's smoothest channel
The pensive poet in his drooping age Might wish to place his reed-roofed hermitage
The pensive poet in his drooping age Might wish to place his reed-roofed hermitage
If fortune smiled not on his early way
If fortune smiled not on his early way
a hard world his errors scanned severe , When late the earth
our careless days , Vacant and glad

Him I remember , in our careless days , Vacant and glad , till many a loss severe First hung his placid eyelids with a tear ; Yet on such visions ardent would he gaze , As the Muse loved , that oft would smile and die , Like the faint bow that leaves the weeping sky ; His heart unguarded , yet it proudly beat Against hard wrong , or coward cold deceit ; — Nor passed he ever without a sigh the cell Where wretchedness and her pale children dwell
Like the faint bow that leaves the weeping sky
heart unguarded , yet it proudly beat Against hard wrong , or coward cold deceit

steals the verdure from the hill

beautiful amid the beams of day
